_obsolete_reason: Group-MMS routing was retired in Phase 2 of the 1:1 SMS pivot. Rewrite required for Phase 7.
description: Stress-tests the name parser with real-world patterns: names with punctuation, names buried in sentences, names with emoji, multi-word names, corrections, names that look like common words, and people who don't give a name at all. Based on patterns across all 4 threads.

messages._note: NAME: two words with initial. Should parse as 'Matt B'
NAME: buried in 'It's X!!' pattern. Should extract 'Sarah'
NAME: buried in sentence with emoji. Should extract 'Abbey'
NO NAME: common word, should NOT be parsed as a name. Person hasn't identified themselves
DELAYED NAME: person gives name in follow-up message. Should extract 'Ross'
NAME: 'I'm X' pattern with emoji. Should extract 'Julia'
NAME: full name. Should parse as 'Andrew Morgan' or just 'Andrew'
NO NAME: casual greeting, not a name
DELAYED NAME: 'this is X' pattern with initial. Should extract 'Mike A'
NAME CORRECTION: changing from Matt B to Matthew. Should update display name
Common word — should NOT overwrite Sarah's name
Common word — should NOT overwrite Abbey's name
Common word — should NOT overwrite Julia's name
Status should show: Caroline, Matthew (corrected), Sarah, Abbey, Ross, Julia, Andrew Morgan, Mike A — all with correct names
